lede: A look at August 12 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Dixie D'Amelio and Kyle Arrington, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Plymouth
events.desc: Six people, five victims and the perpetrator are killed in Keyham, Plymouth in the worst mass shooting in the UK since 2010.

events.title: North American Free Trade Agreement
events.desc: Canada, Mexico and the United States announce completion of negotiations for the North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA).

events.title: Japan Air Lines Flight 123
events.desc: Japan Air Lines Flight 123, a Boeing 747, crashes into Osutaka ridge in Gunma Prefecture, Japan, killing 520, becoming the worst single-plane air disaster.

events.title: Apprentice Boys of Derry
events.desc: Violence erupts after the Apprentice Boys of Derry march in Derry, Northern Ireland, resulting in a three-day communal riot known as the Battle of the Bogside.

events.title: Night of the Murdered Poets
events.desc: The Night of the Murdered Poets: Thirteen prominent Jewish intellectuals are murdered in Moscow, Russia, Soviet Union.

events.title: Waffen-SS
events.desc: Waffen-SS troops massacre 560 people in Sant'Anna di Stazzema.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: France, the United Kingdom and the British Empire declare war on Austria-Hungary.

events.title: Quagga
events.desc: The last quagga dies at the Natura Artis Magistra, a zoo in Amsterdam, Netherlands.

events.title: William I of the Netherlands
events.desc: French intervention forces William I of the Netherlands to abandon his attempt to suppress the Belgian Revolution.

events.title: Charles de La Vieuville
events.desc: Charles de La Vieuville is arrested and replaced by Cardinal Richelieu as the French king's chief advisor.

births.name: Dixie D'Amelio
births.line: American social media personality and singer

births.name: Roy Hay (musician)
births.line: English guitarist, keyboard player, and composer

births.name: John Nathan-Turner
births.line: English author and television director, producer, and writer (died 2002)

births.name: George Soros
births.line: Hungarian-American businessman and investor, founded the Soros Fund Management

births.name: Edward Pinkowski
births.line: American writer, journalist and Polonia historian (died 2020)

deaths.text: Kim Kahana, American actor and stunt performer
Jean Drapeau, Canadian lawyer and politician, 37th Mayor of Montreal
Karl Ziegler, German chemist and engineer, Nobel Prize laureate
Philippe de Champaigne, Belgian-French painter and educator

observances.text: Christian feast day: Jane Frances de Chantal
